Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-17 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker Today's data shows TNGX dominating the momentum rankings with a composite score of 98.5, driven by extraordinary returns that far exceed broad market indices—up 849% over one year versus SPY's 19.36% and QQQ's 25.94%. The consistency across timeframes is remarkable, with all five ratio checks passing and all four moving average gates crossed, indicating sustained upward pressure rather than a spike. LINC ranks second with a 98.0 score and more moderate but still impressive returns (163.68% one-year), though it shows slightly weaker ratio consistency at 4 of 5 passes and a notably higher bad-spy-score of 2.49 versus TNGX's 1.32, suggesting LINC may be more vulnerable during market downturns. Both leaders maintain tight outperformance versus defensive assets like consumer staples (XLP correlation of -0.254 for TNGX) and industrials, indicating their strength is concentrated in growth-oriented positioning rather than broad market beta. The data reflects a bifurcated market where select names have generated outsized momentum independent of traditional sector rotation.
